text: Risk management is an important process for any business or project. It begins with identifying risks, which can be internal, like technical delays, or external, such as changing market conditions. Once risks are identified, teams create mitigation strategies to reduce the chance of problems. For example, they might improve training or use better equipment. However, some risks may still happen, so contingency plans are necessary. These are backup actions, like using a different supplier, if things go wrong. Finally, monitoring is crucial. Regularly reviewing risks helps teams notice new threats early and adjust their plans. Good risk management keeps projects on track and avoids surprises.
